Jacana_greet.Jacana: And that is why the world must change.

Zak_intro.Narrator:
A priestess kneels among a cluster of hollow-eyed and filthy children.  Her purple robes are in tatters, almost to the point of indecency.
			 She seems to have torn strips from her own vestment to bandage a boy's wounds
Zak_intro.Jacana: May it be the hour you first believed
Zak_intro.ZAKARIAH: Oh, this is marvelous. This is simply marvelous. I arrive to find a nun administering the opiate.

It is a drug, Sister. A soporific. A substance which deadens pain by deadening the mind along with it. Which is precisely what you're doing. 
			(to the children, not unkindly but relentlessly) Your fathers died because this city's defenses were managed according to principles laid down three hundred years ago by men who had never seen a Skaarsgaardian raiding formation. 
			It is a logistics problem. It is a solvable logistics problem, and the Senate has the solution sitting in a drawer somewhere because the man who wrote it also wrote an essay arguing that church tithes were a form of taxation without representation
Zak_intro.Jacana: something to do with taking the orphans to the soup kitchen.  Being fed turtle soup.  Jacana knows

Federica_intro.narrator:
The churchyard reeks of fresh-turned earth. Between the headstones, 
			two figures work by lantern-light — a broad-shouldered half-orc woman and
			 a slack-jawed man driving his shovel with mechanical patience. Neither has seen you yet.
Federica_intro.Surgeon-Captain Federica: Deeper, Verhoeven.  No martyr of the antique world lies in a coffin this shallow.
Federica_intro.Verhoeven:
'Outstanding, Ma'am!'  
			**He rolls the casket they've uneartherd on its side and tirelessly resumes his digging beneath its resting place**

choices.text: What Ho, Graverobbers? Speak quickly the reason I should not cry for the guard.
**Pass by quietly, hoping the plunderers don't see you witnessing their crime.**

Federica-intro-talk-down.Surgeon-Captain Federica:
(**freezing mid-crouch, she peers up at you from the shallow grave.  Her companion does not stop digging**)
You would be within your rights. I won't insult you with denials — the evidence is plain.
Two foreign strangers desecrating graves — in wartime no less. I'd expect a speedy trial and an expeditious execution.
**You spot a weapon of unusual shape hanging from her belt, which her hand begins to drift to.** 'That is, if there are any witnesses to testify.'

choices.text: **Back away quietly with your hands raised**.

Federica-intro-spiel.Surgeon-Captain Federica: (**freezing mid-crouch, she peers up at you from the shallow grave.  Her companion does not stop digging**)
Stand easy, we are not thieves.
We perform a service for the living, though it requires a brief discourtesy to the dead.

choice.text: Not to be presumptous, but I suspect you could perhaps instruct me in the noble art of necromancy?

Federica-intro-fight.Surgeon-Captain Federica: If you possess any familiarity with my art, then I'm afraid you've seen too much. [[start_combat(game.characters_in_scene)]]

Federica-intro-apprenticeship.Surgeon-Captain Federica: I am, in fact, in dire need of an apprentice.  And you seem an eager pupil.
Certainly I can teach you.  **She hands you a shovel**. The first, and, I suppose most fundamental lesson, is how to dig a hole.

Federica-intro-agree-to-help.narrator: Your new master imparts a few pointers in the use of a shovel, and you join Verhoeven, who has been digging mechanically this whole time.

The first coffin yields a woman in corroded plate armor — a knight-commander, by the heraldry.
			Federica kneels beside the remains, produces a leather case of surgical instruments, and inserts three long needles into precise points along the skull.
The corpse convulses.  A wet shriek whistles through a throat half-collapsed with rot.
Federica-intro-agree-to-help.THE KNIGHT: (thrashing, clawing at his own ruined face) — it BURNS — what have you —
Federica-intro-agree-to-help.Surgeon Captain Federica: You served under General Maratesta. You campaigned against Emperor Regalus in the Year of Broken Standards.
His weaknesses. His habits in battle. Speak and I will let you sleep again.
Federica-intro-agree-to-help.THE KNIGHT: He... he never took the field himself. Always proxies. We never saw him bleed. Not once. We thought it cowardice. **The rotten limbs begin to thrash harder.**
Federica-intro-agree-to-help.narrator: Federica withdraws the needles. The corpse drops like a puppet cut from its strings. She puts a jack boot on its chest and wrenches both its arms loose from the torso, placing these in a large canvas bag that smells of rot and hay. [[Federica.add_item_to_inventory('Arms of Octavian')]]

choices.text: **You've seen enough to know whatever they're doing isn't ethical.  You shout for the guard.**
**You decide to keep playing along, out of morbid curiousity**
**You decide to keep aiding the Surgeon-Captain in her task, believing the rigteousness of her cause justifies this callous cruelty. The three of you continue digging.**

Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.narrator: The second coffin holds a robed man — a court astrologer, judging by the celestial charts crumbling against his chest. The needles go in. He wakes screaming.
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.THE ASTROLOGER: MY HANDS — I can't feel my — no, I CAN feel them, I can feel them ROTTING —
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.Surgeon-Captain Federica: You served in the court of Regalus. You were executed for treason. Why?
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.THE ASTROLOGER: (shuddering, jaw clicking loose and resetting with each word) I cast his... his true horoscope. Not the man's. The thing beneath the man. The Living Stars do not lie
Fire. He IS fire. A creature from the First Age.  But even he has a touch of impurity.  A drop of human blood.  He is not timeless, he...
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.narrator:
His jaw detaches fully. Federica catches it, examines it briefly, then sets it aside and withdraws the needles. 
					She puts her hands on the thin vertebrae of the scholar's neck and snaps off the rest of his head, tossing it in her bag. [[Federica.add_item_to_inventory('Skull of a Scholar')]]

By now you are over twenty feet deep in the same plot. The third coffin you strike is older than the others — black oak, iron-banded, buried deep. Verhoeven's shovel cracks the lid and something inside cracks back.
The lid explodes upward. A skeleton sits bolt upright in the ruined casket wearing the rotted vestments of a Scarlattian senator. Its jaw unhinges with a sound like tearing leather.
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.THE SENATOR: Heavy is the heart that releases me again upon the world.
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.narrator:
It scrambles from the hole with terrible, insectile speed and vanishes over the churchyard wall into darkness.
					Then, torchlight at the cemetery gates, and the clatter of armor from the lane. [[spawn_character_from_name('golden_guard',4)]]
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.Centurion: Halt! Stand where you are — all of you!
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.narrator: Four city guards pour through the churchyard gate, halberds leveled. They surround the open grave in which the three of you stand, caked in dirt.
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.Centurion: Graverobbers and a gods-damned necromancer. On your knees, the lot of you.
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.Surgeon-Captain Federica: (quietly, to you) I will not kneel. That is not negotiable. What you do next, that is your affair.
Federica-intro-interrogation-continued.Verhoeven: (setting down his shovel with great care, as though placing a child in a crib) Outstanding, Ma'am.

Federica-intro-guard-fight.narrator: A night patrol comes running in response to your call. [[spawn_character_from_name('golden_guard',4)]]

Federica-intro-let-go.Surgeon Captain Federica: **The scowl on her face suggests she thinks your cowardice genuine, and it disgusts her.  She does not pursue you, but picks up her shovel with renewed urgency.**

guard_greet.guard: Halt! Who goes there? State your business.

choices.text: I'm just a traveler passing through.
My name is [[game.protagonist.name]], on official business for the Jarl.

guard_traveler.guard: Is that so? We've had trouble with *travelers* lately.
Move along, but I'm watching you.

guard_persuade_success.guard: The Jarl? Ah, I see the **Jarl's sigil** on your cloak.
Very well, [[game.protagonist.name]]. Go on in. Don't cause any trouble.

guard_persuade_failure.guard: The Jarl? Hmm... you don't look like his usual company.
I don't believe you. Move along!

end_convo.narrator: The guard nods and you walk away.

end_convo_watched.narrator: The guard watches you suspiciously as you leave.

end_convo_pass.narrator: The guard steps aside and lets you pass through the gate.

merchant_trade.merchant: Welcome, welcome! I am **Gareth the Trader**.
I have *rare goods* from across the realm, [[protagonist.name]]!

merchant_weapons.merchant: Ah, a warrior! I have some *fine blades* here.
Each one has tasted **victory** in battle!

merchant_potions.merchant: Indeed! Healing draughts, mana elixirs, even some exotic brews.

merchant_browse.merchant: Of course, of course! Take your time.
Let me know if anything interests you.

merchant_end.narrator: The merchant waves as you depart.

skill_check_example.rogue: Think you can pick this lock?

lockpick_failure.narrator: The picks slip. The lock remains shut.
lockpick_failure.rogue: Don't worry. We'll find another way in.

complex_branch.wizard: Only those worthy may possess it.

worthy_path.wizard: You bear the **Elder Seal**! You are indeed worthy.
worthy_path.narrator: The wizard hands you the ancient tome.

knowledge_path.wizard: Your knowledge of the arcane is impressive.
Perhaps you are worthy after all...
knowledge_path.narrator: The wizard reluctantly gives you the tome.

unworthy_path.wizard: You are *not* ready. Return when you have proven yourself.
